behaviors.description: The generated function signature matches the expected interface (parameter names, types, return type) specified in the description.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Unit beats Integration because core check is signature correctness, not module handoff; Unit beats System because no real OS/network/subprocess is required; Agentic beats Unit because correctness depends on real LLM interpretation of natural-language interface requirements. Rejected Workflow: behavior text covers a single generation outcome, not a multi-step agent chain.
behaviors.test_description: Run real model generation from interface-heavy prompts (including tricky type/parameter constraints), parse produced signature, and assert exact match to expected interface schema.

behaviors.description: Orchestration executes attempts in order and returns immediately on the first passing attempt.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Integration beats Unit because failure appears at orchestration boundary between attempt loop, test-result ingestion, and stop condition; Integration beats System because deterministic in-process fakes can reproduce ordering/early-return bugs without real OS/network/subprocess. Rejected Agentic: behavior is about control flow, not model-output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Wire orchestrator with deterministic stub generator and stub test runner that fails then passes; assert attempts are invoked strictly in order and third attempt is never called after first pass.

behaviors.description: For a function that a single call usually fails, the live iterative loop can still produce a passing implementation within the allowed attempts.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Integration beats Unit because this is loop orchestration across components; System does not win because real OS primitives are not the defining risk; Agentic beats Integration because success depends on real model output changes; Workflow beats Agentic because behavior explicitly requires sequential agentic steps (generate -> fail -> refine -> pass).
behaviors.test_description: Execute end-to-end with real LLM on a benchmark prompt known to fail first attempt; run full retry loop and assert a later attempt within limit passes provided tests.

behaviors.description: When tests fail, the refinement prompt builder includes the failed test names and failure messages in the next LLM prompt.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Integration beats Unit because real bug is often handoff/serialization between test-result collector and prompt builder; Integration beats System because this can be caught with in-process fixtures; rejected Agentic because behavior text targets prompt-content wiring, not whether model responds well to that content.
behaviors.test_description: Run failing test execution through real result-to-prompt pipeline, capture constructed refinement prompt, and assert it contains exact failed test identifiers and failure messages.

behaviors.description: The next live attempt changes implementation content in response to prior failure context rather than resubmitting the same code.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Integration beats Unit because context must flow across attempt boundaries; System does not win because OS/network realism is not the primary failure boundary; Agentic beats Integration because change quality depends on real model behavior; Workflow beats Agentic because behavior requires multiple agentic steps in sequence and correct handoff from prior failure context to next generation.
behaviors.test_description: Use real LLM with two-attempt loop where first attempt fails; verify second generated implementation is materially different and addresses referenced failure context rather than repeating prior code.

behaviors.description: The service stops after 3 failed attempts and returns a structured error with all three attempts and their test results.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Integration beats Unit because failure can occur in cross-module attempt bookkeeping + error assembly; Integration beats System because deterministic local stubs can force three failures and validate structure. Rejected Agentic: behavior checks retry cap and error payload shape, not model judgment quality.
behaviors.test_description: With stub generator and stub test executor always failing, run orchestrator and assert exactly 3 attempts occur and returned error payload includes per-attempt code/test-result records for all three.

behaviors.description: The full non-agentic pipeline wiring (generate call, test execution, attempt bookkeeping) works end to end with deterministic stubbed model outputs.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Integration beats Unit because this is full pipeline wiring; System beats Integration because end-to-end test execution path commonly includes real subprocess/filesystem behavior that in-process fakes can miss. Rejected Agentic per behavior text: "non-agentic" and deterministic stubs explicitly remove model-quality dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Run service end-to-end with deterministic model stub but real code execution harness (same runner/process mode as production); assert generation ingestion, test execution, and bookkeeping are consistent across attempts.

behaviors.description: The full live loop (generate, test, refine, retest) solves at least one known hard function that fails in the first attempt.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Integration beats Unit because behavior spans multiple modules; System is insufficient alone because primary risk is model-driven improvement quality across retries; Agentic beats Integration due real LLM dependence; Workflow beats Agentic because behavior explicitly requires chained agentic steps and successful multi-step recovery after first-attempt failure.
behaviors.test_description: Maintain a curated hard-function case where first live attempt is expected to fail; run real loop through retries and assert eventual pass within limit, recording per-step artifacts for regression tracking.
